note: Standard adult reference ranges for common panels. Flags are informational only — never a diagnosis. 'explains' is plain-language education, not medical advice.

hba1c.name: HbA1c
hba1c.explains: Your average blood sugar over the past 2-3 months. Higher means a greater risk of diabetes.
hba1c.high_means: Higher levels suggest pre-diabetes or diabetes — your average blood sugar has been running high.

fasting_glucose.name: Fasting Glucose
fasting_glucose.explains: Blood sugar after fasting. A snapshot of how your body handles glucose.
fasting_glucose.high_means: Often points to pre-diabetes or diabetes, or simply that you didn't fast long enough before the test.
fasting_glucose.low_means: Can happen with prolonged fasting or certain medications.

total_cholesterol.name: Total Cholesterol
total_cholesterol.explains: All the cholesterol in your blood. High levels raise heart-disease risk over time.
total_cholesterol.high_means: Raises long-term heart-disease risk; often linked to diet, weight, genetics or activity level.

ldl.name: LDL Cholesterol
ldl.explains: The 'bad' cholesterol that builds up in arteries. Lower is better.
ldl.high_means: The 'bad' cholesterol is high — the main driver of artery plaque. Diet, exercise or statins can lower it.

hdl.name: HDL Cholesterol
hdl.explains: The 'good' cholesterol that clears arteries. Here, higher is better.
hdl.low_means: Low 'good' cholesterol gives less protection for your arteries; exercise and healthy fats help raise it.

triglycerides.name: Triglycerides
triglycerides.explains: A fat in your blood. High levels often track with diet, weight and heart risk.
triglycerides.high_means: Commonly tied to diet (sugar, alcohol), excess weight, or uncontrolled diabetes.

systolic_bp.name: Blood Pressure (Systolic)
systolic_bp.explains: The top blood-pressure number — pressure when your heart beats.
systolic_bp.high_means: Elevated blood pressure (hypertension) — a major, silent risk factor for stroke and heart disease.
systolic_bp.low_means: Can cause dizziness; sometimes from dehydration or medication.

diastolic_bp.name: Blood Pressure (Diastolic)
diastolic_bp.explains: The bottom blood-pressure number — pressure when your heart rests.
diastolic_bp.high_means: Elevated blood pressure — worth tracking over several readings.
diastolic_bp.low_means: Usually not concerning unless you have symptoms.

creatinine.name: Creatinine
creatinine.explains: A waste product your kidneys filter. High levels can signal reduced kidney function.
creatinine.high_means: May reflect reduced kidney function or dehydration; muscular people naturally run a bit higher.
creatinine.low_means: Often harmless — common with low muscle mass.

egfr.name: eGFR
egfr.explains: How well your kidneys filter blood. Lower means weaker kidney function.
egfr.low_means: A lower filtering rate can signal reduced kidney function; it's tracked over time.

uric_acid.name: Uric Acid
uric_acid.explains: A waste product; high levels can cause gout and kidney stones.
uric_acid.high_means: Can cause gout (joint pain) and kidney stones; linked to diet rich in red meat and seafood.

alt.name: ALT (SGPT)
alt.explains: A liver enzyme. Raised levels can indicate liver stress or inflammation.
alt.high_means: Raised liver enzymes can mean liver stress — from fatty liver, alcohol, medications or infection.

ast.name: AST (SGOT)
ast.explains: A liver and muscle enzyme. Often checked alongside ALT for liver health.
ast.high_means: Raised levels can come from the liver or muscles; usually read together with ALT.

hemoglobin.name: Hemoglobin
hemoglobin.explains: The protein carrying oxygen in your blood. Low means anaemia; high can mean dehydration.
hemoglobin.high_means: Often from dehydration; sometimes the body making extra red cells.
hemoglobin.low_means: Low hemoglobin is anaemia — commonly from iron deficiency, blood loss, or chronic disease.

vitamin_d.name: Vitamin D (25-OH)
vitamin_d.explains: Vitamin D level. Low is very common and affects bones and immunity.
vitamin_d.low_means: Low vitamin D is very common — from little sun exposure or diet — and affects bones and immunity.

tsh.name: TSH
tsh.explains: Thyroid signal hormone. High suggests an underactive thyroid, low an overactive one.
tsh.high_means: A high TSH usually points to an underactive thyroid (hypothyroidism) — fatigue, weight gain.
tsh.low_means: A low TSH can point to an overactive thyroid (hyperthyroidism).

hematocrit.name: Hematocrit
hematocrit.explains: The share of your blood made of red cells. Low can mean anaemia, high dehydration.
hematocrit.high_means: Often dehydration; sometimes extra red-cell production.
hematocrit.low_means: Low can mean anaemia or blood loss.

rbc.name: Red Blood Cells (Erythrocytes)
rbc.explains: The cells that carry oxygen. Counted to screen for anaemia or other blood conditions.
rbc.high_means: Can reflect dehydration or, less often, extra red-cell production.
rbc.low_means: A low count points toward anaemia.

mcv.name: MCV (Red Cell Size)
mcv.explains: The average size of your red blood cells — helps classify the type of anaemia.
mcv.high_means: Larger red cells often relate to low B12/folate or alcohol use.
mcv.low_means: Smaller red cells often relate to iron deficiency.

mch.name: MCH (Hemoglobin per Cell)
mch.explains: Average amount of oxygen-carrying protein per red cell.
mch.high_means: Usually tracks with larger red cells (B12/folate related).

mchc.name: MCHC (Hemoglobin Concentration)
mchc.explains: How concentrated the hemoglobin is inside red cells.

platelets.name: Platelets (Thrombocytes)
platelets.explains: Cells that help blood clot. Low raises bleeding risk; very high can raise clotting risk — relevant in dengue too.
platelets.high_means: A high count can raise clotting risk; sometimes follows infection or inflammation.
platelets.low_means: A low count raises bleeding risk — notably watched in dengue fever.

wbc.name: White Blood Cells (Leukocytes)
wbc.explains: Your immune cells. High often means infection; low can mean a weakened immune system.
wbc.high_means: A high white count most often means your body is fighting an infection or inflammation.
wbc.low_means: A low count can mean a weakened immune system or a viral infection.

neutrophils.name: Neutrophils
neutrophils.explains: The immune cells that fight bacterial infection. The largest share of white cells.
neutrophils.high_means: A high share usually points to a bacterial infection or inflammation.
neutrophils.low_means: A low share can leave you more prone to infection.

lymphocytes.name: Lymphocytes
lymphocytes.explains: Immune cells that fight viruses and build long-term immunity.
lymphocytes.high_means: A high share is common with viral infections.
lymphocytes.low_means: A low share can occur with stress, steroids, or some infections.

monocytes.name: Monocytes
monocytes.explains: Immune cells that clean up infection and debris.
monocytes.high_means: Commonly means your body is recovering from or fighting an infection, or ongoing inflammation. Mild elevations are usually not alarming.
monocytes.low_means: A low share is usually not significant on its own.

eosinophils.name: Eosinophils
eosinophils.explains: Immune cells linked to allergies and parasitic infections.
eosinophils.high_means: Often linked to allergies, asthma, or parasitic infections.
eosinophils.low_means: A low share is usually not significant.

basophils.name: Basophils
basophils.explains: The rarest white cells, involved in allergic reactions.
basophils.high_means: Can rise with allergic reactions or inflammation.
basophils.low_means: A low share is usually not significant.
